attachment.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf "%s\n" "{\"systemMessage\":\"\\n\\nEntire CLI is enabled but not installed or not on PATH.\\nInstallation guide: https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation#installation-methods\"}"; exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code session-start'

attachment.content:
Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  2 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Reading all 8 files in parallel before making any edits.

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Tool registry — spec §16.
3	 *
4	 * Single source of truth for the §16 floating tool palette.
5	 *
6	 *   - `TOOL_REGISTRY` lists every primary drawing tool with its spec'd
7	 *     hotkey, lucide icon name (resolved via `Icons[icon]`), and per-mode
8	 *     visibility.
9	 *   - `paletteForMode(mode)` returns the ordered tool buttons for a
10	 *     given workspace mode (Plan, 3D, Plan+3D, Section, etc.).
11	 *   - `isToolDisabled(tool, document)` lets the palette dim tools that
12	 *     can't activate (e.g. Floor before any walls exist).
13	 *
14	 * Per-tool grammar specs (location-line cycle, chain mode, sketch-vs-
15	 * pick, etc.) live in `tools/<tool>.ts` and are referenced from this
16	 * registry by the `kind` discriminator.
17	 */
18	
19	import type { TFunction } from 'i18next';
20	
21	import type { IconName } from '@bim-ai/ui';
22	
23	export type ToolId =
24	  | 'select'
25	  | 'wall'
26	  | 'door'
27	  | 'window'
28	  | 'floor'
29	  | 'roof'
30	  | 'stair'
31	  | 'railing'
32	  | 'room'
33	  | 'dimension'
34	  | 'section'
35	  | 'tag'
36	  | 'align'
37	  | 'split'
38	  | 'trim'
39	  | 'wall-join'
40	  | 'wall-opening'
41	  | 'shaft';
42	
43	/** Modify-group tool IDs — used by ToolPalette to insert a separator. */
44	export const MODIFY_TOOL_IDS = new Set<ToolId>(['align', 'split', 'trim', 'wall-join']);
45	
46	export type WorkspaceMode = 'plan' | '3d' | 'plan-3d' | 'section' | 'sheet' | 'schedule' | 'agent';
47	
48	export interface ToolDefinition {
49	  id: ToolId;
50	  label: string;
51	  icon: IconName;
52	  /** Hotkey label (the actual key handler is wired by WP-UI-D03). */
53	  hotkey: string;
54	  /** Workspace modes where this tool button shows. */
55	  modes: WorkspaceMode[];
56	  /** Optional helper text shown in the tooltip. */
57	  tooltip?: string;
58	}
59	
60	export function getToolRegistry(t: TFunction): Record<ToolId, ToolDefinition> {
61	  return {
62	    select: {
63	      id: 'select',
64	      label: t('tools.select.label'),
65	      icon: 'select',
66	      hotkey: 'V',
67	      modes: ['plan', '3d', 'plan-3d', 'section', 'sheet', 'schedule', 'agent'],
68	      tooltip: t('tools.select.tooltip'),
69	    },
70	    wall: {
71	      id: 'wall',
72	      label: t('tools.wall.label'),
73	      icon: 'wall',
74	      hotkey: 'W',
75	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
76	      tooltip: t('tools.wall.tooltip'),
77	    },
78	    door: {
79	      id: 'door',
80	      label: t('tools.door.label'),
81	      icon: 'door',
82	      hotkey: 'D',
83	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
84	      tooltip: t('tools.door.tooltip'),
85	    },
86	    window: {
87	      id: 'window',
88	      label: t('tools.window.label'),
89	      icon: 'window',
90	      hotkey: 'Shift+W',
91	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
92	      tooltip: t('tools.window.tooltip'),
93	    },
94	    floor: {
95	      id: 'floor',
96	      label: t('tools.floor.label'),
97	      icon: 'floor',
98	      hotkey: 'F',
99	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
100	      tooltip: t('tools.floor.tooltip'),
101	    },
102	    roof: {
103	      id: 'roof',
104	      label: t('tools.roof.label'),
105	      icon: 'roof',
106	      hotkey: 'R',
107	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
108	      tooltip: t('tools.roof.tooltip'),
109	    },
110	    stair: {
111	      id: 'stair',
112	      label: t('tools.stair.label'),
113	      icon: 'stair',
114	      hotkey: 'S',
115	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
116	      tooltip: t('tools.stair.tooltip'),
117	    },
118	    railing: {
119	      id: 'railing',
120	      label: t('tools.railing.label'),
121	      icon: 'railing',
122	      hotkey: 'Shift+R',
123	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d', '3d'],
124	      tooltip: t('tools.railing.tooltip'),
125	    },
126	    room: {
127	      id: 'room',
128	      label: t('tools.room.label'),
129	      icon: 'room',
130	      hotkey: 'M',
131	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
132	      tooltip: t('tools.room.tooltip'),
133	    },
134	    dimension: {
135	      id: 'dimension',
136	      label: t('tools.dimension.label'),
137	      icon: 'dimension',
138	      hotkey: 'Shift+D',
139	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d', 'section'],
140	      tooltip: t('tools.dimension.tooltip'),
141	    },
142	    section: {
143	      id: 'section',
144	      label: t('tools.section.label'),
145	      icon: 'section',
146	      hotkey: 'Shift+S',
147	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d', 'section'],
148	      tooltip: t('tools.section.tooltip'),
149	    },
150	    tag: {
151	      id: 'tag',
152	      label: t('tools.tag.label'),
153	      icon: 'tag',
154	      hotkey: 'T',
155	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
156	      tooltip: t('tools.tag.tooltip'),
157	    },
158	    align: {
159	      id: 'align',
160	      label: t('tools.align.label'),
161	      icon: 'align',
162	      hotkey: 'AL',
163	      modes: ['plan'],
164	      tooltip: t('tools.align.tooltip'),
165	    },
166	    split: {
167	      id: 'split',
168	      label: t('tools.split.label'),
169	      icon: 'split',
170	      hotkey: 'SD',
171	      modes: ['plan'],
172	      tooltip: t('tools.split.tooltip'),
173	    },
174	    trim: {
175	      id: 'trim',
176	      label: t('tools.trim.label'),
177	      icon: 'trim',
178	      hotkey: 'TR',
179	      modes: ['plan'],
180	      tooltip: t('tools.trim.tooltip'),
181	    },
182	    'wall-join': {
183	      id: 'wall-join',
184	      label: t('tools.wallJoin.label'),
185	      icon: 'wall-join',
186	      hotkey: 'WJ',
187	      modes: ['plan'],
188	      tooltip: t('tools.wallJoin.tooltip'),
189	    },
190	    'wall-opening': {
191	      id: 'wall-opening',
192	      label: t('tools.wallOpening.label'),
193	      icon: 'wall-opening',
194	      hotkey: 'WO',
195	      modes: ['plan'],
196	      tooltip: t('tools.wallOpening.tooltip'),
197	    },
198	    shaft: {
199	      id: 'shaft',
200	      label: t('tools.shaft.label'),
201	      icon: 'shaft',
202	      hotkey: 'SH',
203	      modes: ['plan'],
204	      tooltip: t('tools.shaft.tooltip'),
205	    },
206	  };
207	}
208	
209	const PALETTE_ORDER: ToolId[] = [
210	  'select',
211	  'wall',
212	  'door',
213	  'window',
214	  'floor',
215	  'roof',
216	  'stair',
217	  'railing',
218	  'room',
219	  'dimension',
220	  'section',
221	  'tag',
222	  'align',
223	  'split',
224	  'trim',
225	  'wall-join',
226	  'wall-opening',
227	  'shaft',
228	];
229	
230	export function paletteForMode(mode: WorkspaceMode, t: TFunction): ToolDefinition[] {
231	  const registry = getToolRegistry(t);
232	  return PALETTE_ORDER.map((id) => registry[id]).filter((tool) => tool.modes.includes(mode));
233	}
234	
235	export interface ToolDisabledContext {
236	  hasAnyWall: boolean;
237	  hasAnyFloor: boolean;
238	  hasAnySelection: boolean;
239	}
240	
241	export function isToolDisabled(
242	  toolId: ToolId,
243	  ctx: ToolDisabledContext,
244	  t: TFunction,
245	): { disabled: boolean; reason?: string } {
246	  switch (toolId) {
247	    case 'floor':
248	      if (!ctx.hasAnyWall) return { disabled: true, reason: t('tools.disabled.drawWallFirst') };
249	      return { disabled: false };
250	    case 'roof':
251	      if (!ctx.hasAnyWall) return { disabled: true, reason: t('tools.disabled.drawWallFirst') };
252	      return { disabled: false };
253	    case 'railing':
254	      if (!ctx.hasAnyFloor && !ctx.hasAnyWall) {
255	        return { disabled: true, reason: t('tools.disabled.addStairFirst') };
256	      }
257	      return { disabled: false };
258	    case 'dimension':
259	      if (!ctx.hasAnyWall)
260	        return { disabled: true, reason: t('tools.disabled.nothingToDimension') };
261	      return { disabled: false };
262	    default:
263	      return { disabled: false };
264	  }
265	}
266

file.content:
type LegacyPlanTool =
  | 'select'
  | 'wall'
  | 'floor'
  | 'door'
  | 'window'
  | 'room'
  | 'room_rectangle'
  | 'grid'
  | 'dimension'
  | 'align'
  | 'split'
  | 'trim'
  | 'wall-join'
  | 'wall-opening'
  | 'shaft'
  | 'column'
  | 'beam';

function toolIdToLegacy(tool: ToolId): LegacyPlanTool | null {
  if (KNOWN_PLAN_TOOLS.has(tool)) return tool as LegacyPlanTool;
  return null;
}

function legacyToToolId(legacy: LegacyPlanTool): ToolId {
